title: A Pastoral
content:
آ آ آ آ Just as the sun was setting
آ آ آ آ Back of the Western hills
آ آ آ آ Grandfather stood by the window
آ آ آ آ Eating the last of his pills.

آ آ آ آ And Grandmother, by the cupboard,
آ آ آ آ Knitting, heard him say:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½I ought to have went to the village
آ آ آ آ To fetch some more pills today.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then Grandmother snuffled a teardrop
آ آ آ آ And said. ï؟½It is jest like I suz
آ آ آ آ Tï؟½ thï؟½ parsonï؟½Grandfatherï؟½s liver
آ آ آ آ Ainï؟½t what it used to was:

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Itï؟½s gittinï؟½ torpid and dormant,
آ آ آ آ It donï؟½t function like of old,
آ آ آ آ And even them pills he swallers
آ آ آ آ Donï؟½t seem no more tï؟½ catch hold;

آ آ آ آ ï؟½They used to grab it and shake it
آ آ آ آ And joggle it up and down
آ آ آ آ And turn dear Grandfather yaller
آ آ آ آ Except when they turned him brown;

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I remember when we was married
آ آ آ آ His liver was lively and gay,
آ آ آ آ A kickinï؟½ anï؟½ rippinï؟½ anï؟½ givinï؟½
آ آ آ آ Dear Ezry new pains evï؟½ry day;

آ آ آ آ ï؟½It used to turn clear over backwards
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ palpitate wussï؟½n a pump
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ give him the janders and yallers
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ bounce around thumpty-thump;

آ آ آ آ ï؟½But now it is torpid and dormant
آ آ آ آ And painless and quiet and cold;
آ آ آ آ Ah, me! allï؟½s so peaceful anï؟½ quiet
آ آ آ آ Since Grandfatherï؟½s liver ï؟½s grown old!

آ آ آ آ Then Grandmother wiped a new teardrop
آ آ آ آ And sighed: ï؟½It is just like I suz
آ آ آ آ Tï؟½ thï؟½ parson: Grandfatherï؟½s liver
آ آ آ آ Ainï؟½t what it used to was.ï؟½
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ellis Parker Butler delivers a powerful performance in "A Pastoral"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
